perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Term of Agreement Except as otherwise provided herein, the term of this Agreement is three (3) years from the Execution Date ("Cooperation Period"). The Parties may negotiate for an extension of this Agreement six (6) months before the expiration of the Cooperation Period.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Term of Agreement Except as otherwise provided herein, the term of this Agreement is three (3) years from the Execution Date ("Cooperation Period"). The Parties may terminate this Agreement at any time, with no prior notice required, effectively overriding the initial three-year term.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the term of the agreement. Initially, the agreement is defined as lasting three years, with a provision for extension. The added clause allows for immediate termination at any time, negating the three-year term and creating uncertainty about the actual duration of the agreement. This contradicts the original three-year term specified in Section 2.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Term of Agreement Except as otherwise provided herein, the term of this Agreement is three (3) years from the Execution Date ("Cooperation Period"). The Parties may terminate this Agreement at any time, with no prior notice required, effectively overriding the initial three-year term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for immediate termination of the agreement, effectively overriding and contradicting the initially stated three-year term in Section 2.1. The contradicted text reiterates this clause, highlighting the direct conflict as the agreement can no longer be reliably considered a three-year term due to the termination clause.

perturbation.original_text: 3.9 Confidentiality The Parties agree that the Parties will not disclose information in connection with the execution, existence, content, and performance of this Agreement to any third party before the Parties have made an announcement to the public pursuant to Section 3.11 of this Agreement. However, the foregoing restriction shall not apply to disclosure made to the board of the directors, management team, and relevant employees who need to know such information, attorneys, accountants, financial counsel, and competent authorities for the purposes of performing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.9 Confidentiality Notwithstanding any other provision in this Agreement, the Parties reserve the right to disclose any and all information related to this Agreement to any third party, at their sole discretion, without any prior notice or consent from the other Party. This disclosure right supersedes any other confidentiality obligations outlined in this Agreement. However, the foregoing restriction shall not apply to disclosure made to the board of the directors, management team, and relevant employees who need to know such information, attorneys, accountants, financial counsel, and competent authorities for the purposes of performing this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause completely undermines the original confidentiality clause (3.9). By stating that parties can disclose 'any and all information' to any third party 'at their sole discretion,' it directly contradicts the initial restriction on disclosure to third parties before a public announcement. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the confidentiality obligations under the agreement. This contradicts the initial purpose of confidentiality as previously mentioned in Section 3.9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.9 Confidentiality Notwithstanding any other provision in this Agreement, the Parties reserve the right to disclose any and all information related to this Agreement to any third party, at their sole discretion, without any prior notice or consent from the other Party. This disclosure right supersedes any other confidentiality obligations outlined in this Agreement. However, the foregoing restriction shall not apply to disclosure made to the board of the directors, management team, and relevant employees who need to know such information, attorneys, accountants, financial counsel, and competent authorities for the purposes of performing this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows parties to disclose 'any and all information' to any third party at their discretion. This directly contradicts the original confidentiality clause (3.9) in the original text, which restricted disclosure to third parties before a public announcement. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 3.9.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Assignment of Rights and Obligations Neither Party shall assign any rights or obligations provided herein without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Assignment of Rights and Obligations Either Party may assign all or any portion of its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any affiliate or successor without obtaining the prior written consent of the other Party. However, Neither Party shall assign any rights or obligations provided herein without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a direct contradiction within the clause regarding the assignment of rights and obligations. The clause initially states that neither party can assign rights or obligations without the other party's consent. The added clause states that either party may assign all or any portion of its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any affiliate or successor without obtaining the prior written consent of the other Party, contradicting the original section regarding assignments under Section 3.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Assignment of Rights and Obligations Either Party may assign all or any portion of its rights and obligations under this Agreement to any affiliate or successor without obtaining the prior written consent of the other Party. However, Neither Party shall assign any rights or obligations provided herein without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Initially, the clause states neither party can assign rights without consent. The modification adds that either party *may* assign rights to affiliates without consent, creating a clear conflict with the original restriction. The contradiction is evident because the clause states that assignments are allowed without consent in one part while simultaneously stating that assignments require consent in another.
